channel.title: ConnectBlue Political Ad Tech News
channel.description: Latest news and insights on political advertising technology, campaign ad spending, and ad tech innovation in 2026 elections

item.title: Five Ad Tech Stories to Watch at Cannes
item.description: Cannes Lions 2026 spotlight: Yahoo DSP Agent Network (agentic ecosystem), DoubleVerify DV Neura (AI quality engine integrated with Claude), supply-side measurement Elevate (upstream inventory transparency), OpenAI ad ambitions, and Publicis-LiveRamp identity conflicts. Political campaigns must track these shifts because DSP innovation and platform consolidation directly impact targeting accuracy and cost efficiency.
item.ns1:encoded:
Cannes Lions 2026 spotlight: Yahoo DSP Agent Network (agentic ecosystem), DoubleVerify DV Neura (AI quality engine integrated with Claude), supply-side measurement Elevate (upstream inventory transparency), OpenAI ad ambitions, and Publicis-LiveRamp identity conflicts. Political campaigns must track these shifts because DSP innovation and platform consolidation directly impact targeting accuracy and cost efficiency.

]]>

item.title: BidMachine Introduces Direct Placements and Agentic Readiness
item.description: BidMachine launches protocol-agnostic agentic readiness pilots for mobile DSPs—enabling buyer agents to transact across AdCP, AAMP, and A2A standards with 20+ demand partners already active. For political media buyers, this means agentic mobile campaign buying is production-ready by Q3 2026, shifting how campaigns optimize targeting across device types.
item.ns1:encoded:
BidMachine launches protocol-agnostic agentic readiness pilots for mobile DSPs—enabling buyer agents to transact across AdCP, AAMP, and A2A standards with 20+ demand partners already active. For political media buyers, this means agentic mobile campaign buying is production-ready by Q3 2026, shifting how campaigns optimize targeting across device types.

]]>

item.title: Big Money Flows Into Key Democratic Races Ahead of June Primary
item.description: Colorado Democratic primaries show record super PAC spending—$1M+ in CD-1 alone, with dark money ($2M) fueling state legislative races. This pattern signals that 2026 will see unprecedented non-candidate spending in mid-tier races, requiring campaigns to model interference and dark money opposition well before the general election cycle begins.

item.title: Conservatives Spent Heavily in Key Democratic Primaries, Filings Show
item.description: FEC filings reveal Conservative Americans PAC funded $4.4M in pop-up super PACs with fake "progressive" names to meddle in Democratic primaries and choose weak general election opponents. This is a high-value lesson for political ad buyers: dark money networks can now coordinate at scale to engineer specific matchups and suppress targeted opposition.
item.ns1:encoded:
FEC filings reveal Conservative Americans PAC funded $4.4M in pop-up super PACs with fake "progressive" names to meddle in Democratic primaries and choose weak general election opponents. This is a high-value lesson for political ad buyers: dark money networks can now coordinate at scale to engineer specific matchups and suppress targeted opposition.

]]>

item.title: An AI Proxy War Could Reshape Congress—Before Congress Reshapes AI
item.description: OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, and Google are collectively spending tens of millions on super PACs to shape AI regulation in 2026—creating structural influence over AI policy that will directly impact political advertising platforms and targeting capabilities. Campaigns must understand this funding war is really about control over the regulatory landscape that governs how they reach voters.
item.ns1:encoded:
OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, and Google are collectively spending tens of millions on super PACs to shape AI regulation in 2026—creating structural influence over AI policy that will directly impact political advertising platforms and targeting capabilities. Campaigns must understand this funding war is really about control over the regulatory landscape that governs how they reach voters.

]]>

item.title: Foreign Misinformation Campaigns Assessed to Spike Around 2026 Midterm Elections
item.description: Intelligence officials warn of imminent foreign state disinformation surge (Russia, China, Iran) around 2026 midterms. U.S. counter-disinformation infrastructure degraded. Campaigns must plan for coordinated foreign interference in ad strategies.
item.ns1:encoded:
Intelligence officials warn of imminent foreign state disinformation surge (Russia, China, Iran) around 2026 midterms. U.S. counter-disinformation infrastructure degraded. Campaigns must plan for coordinated foreign interference in ad strategies.

]]>

item.title: AI deepfake media can sway public opinion as effectively as real media, UVU study finds
item.description: UVU research proves deepfakes persuade voters as effectively as authentic video; 60% of voters identify deepfakes as real. Campaigns must deploy media authentication and detection infrastructure.
item.ns1:encoded:
UVU research proves deepfakes persuade voters as effectively as authentic video; 60% of voters identify deepfakes as real. Campaigns must deploy media authentication and detection infrastructure.

]]>

item.title: AI Giants Funding Ad Wars Across the Country
item.description: OpenAI and Anthropic-backed super PACs have spent $37M+ on political ads to influence AI policy races; major tech companies are now direct political advertisers with regulatory capture agendas.
item.ns1:encoded:
OpenAI and Anthropic-backed super PACs have spent $37M+ on political ads to influence AI policy races; major tech companies are now direct political advertisers with regulatory capture agendas.

]]>

item.title: AI deepfake ads invade midterm election campaigns
item.description: Deepfakes are now the dominant attack ad format in 2026 midterms; campaigns without synthetic media defense strategies face credibility vulnerabilities.
item.ns1:encoded:
Deepfakes are now the dominant attack ad format in 2026 midterms; campaigns without synthetic media defense strategies face credibility vulnerabilities.

]]>

item.title: $35M campaign finance fine against Meta upheld by WA Supreme Court
item.description: Washington State Supreme Court upholds $35M fine against Meta for campaign finance disclosure violations. Meta failed to provide required disclosure records for political ads 100+ times. Landmark ruling affirms state disclosure laws and Meta's duty to report ad sponsors, costs, targeting, and audience. Strategic implication: Campaigns must ensure political ad compliance across Meta platforms; state-level disclosure requirements are now enforceable and penalties are material.

item.title: Philo Launches Pause Ads
item.description: Philo goes live with pause ads across full ad-supported footprint, both direct and programmatic (first in industry). Integrated with DSPs (Index, Magnite, Kargo, Nexxen, OpenX, TripleLift, WunderKIND). 81% of viewers pause content to avoid missing it; pause sessions show higher QR engagement and purchase intent. Political campaigns can now activate pause creative via standard DSP workflows without custom integrations.

item.title: Streaming TV Pause Ads Are Ready To Scale—If the Industry Can Standardize
item.description: 80% of buyers expect to increase pause ad spend 20%+ over next year. Publishers ready to monetize but lack standardization; 70% cite fragmentation as barrier. Pricing misalignment: buyers expect <$15 CPM, publishers value at $15–$25+. Political campaigns: pause ads are emerging as high-attention, non-disruptive format for political messaging. IAB standardization efforts underway—winners will be early adopters with technical integration.
item.ns1:encoded:
80% of buyers expect to increase pause ad spend 20%+ over next year. Publishers ready to monetize but lack standardization; 70% cite fragmentation as barrier. Pricing misalignment: buyers expect <$15 CPM, publishers value at $15–$25+. Political campaigns: pause ads are emerging as high-attention, non-disruptive format for political messaging. IAB standardization efforts underway—winners will be early adopters with technical integration.

]]>

item.title: Midterm Ad Discussion Revolves Around Trump
item.description: 55% of House/Senate TV ads mention Trump; 73% of GOP House ads feature the president. Outside groups now outspend candidates 2-to-1 in Senate races ($394M vs $210M). Spending breakdown: 43% broadcast TV, 27% CTV, 15% digital, 12% cable. Critical insight: Messaging strategy is Trump-centric for Republicans; campaigns without Trump-aligned creative risk message misalignment.
item.ns1:encoded:
55% of House/Senate TV ads mention Trump; 73% of GOP House ads feature the president. Outside groups now outspend candidates 2-to-1 in Senate races ($394M vs $210M). Spending breakdown: 43% broadcast TV, 27% CTV, 15% digital, 12% cable. Critical insight: Messaging strategy is Trump-centric for Republicans; campaigns without Trump-aligned creative risk message misalignment.

]]>

item.title: $11B midterm ad race may drive record CPMs in key battlegrounds
item.description: Political ad spending projected to reach $11.07 billion (21.4% increase over 2022). CTV surging to $2.70 billion (194% increase)—campaigns that haven't scaled CTV by Q3 will face inventory premiums and cost disadvantages in battleground states.
item.ns1:encoded:
Political ad spending projected to reach $11.07 billion (21.4% increase over 2022). CTV surging to $2.70 billion (194% increase)—campaigns that haven't scaled CTV by Q3 will face inventory premiums and cost disadvantages in battleground states.

]]>

item.title: Zeta Global Brings Athena Agentic AI to Agency Workflows for Campaign Optimization
item.description: Zeta's Athena AI agents now embed directly into agency workflows, continuously monitoring 245M consumer signals and autonomously recommending (and executing) real-time optimizations across campaigns. Enables 2–3x faster decision-making and measurable performance improvements for political teams managing multiple high-stakes campaigns.

item.title: Gracenote and PubMatic Partner on Content-Level CTV Targeting and Live Sports Deals
item.description: Gracenote's episode-level metadata now integrates into PubMatic's platform, enabling campaigns to align ads with specific shows and live sports events programmatically. Campaigns can now buy premium sports inventory with verified content alignment instead of generic audience-based CTV buys.
item.ns1:encoded:
Gracenote's episode-level metadata now integrates into PubMatic's platform, enabling campaigns to align ads with specific shows and live sports events programmatically. Campaigns can now buy premium sports inventory with verified content alignment instead of generic audience-based CTV buys.

]]>

item.title: Nexxen and L2 Data Launch VoterMatch Political Advertising Solution
item.description: Nexxen DSP + L2 voter data integration enables deterministic (verified) voter exposure measurement instead of probabilistic estimates, reducing wasted spend by 15-20% and enabling real-time frequency optimization across CTV and programmatic political campaigns.

item.title: Gray Media Launches Political 360 With Aristotle Data Integration
item.description: TV broadcaster Gray Media integrates 235M voter records and 267M consumer profiles via Aristotle, enabling precise voter-linked targeting across broadcast and digital channels for political campaigns—unifying reach and targeting in a single voter-linked platform for the first time.

item.title: Ad market reaches its highest share of GDP
item.description: Big Tech platform consolidation accelerating: Google, Meta, Amazon now control 57.6% of global ad revenue (up from 43.8% five years ago). Political campaigns face concentration risk and shrinking negotiating leverage. Diversified media strategy across CTV, DSP, and contextual channels essential to hedge platform dependency and policy risk.

item.title: Scoop: Hightouch offers Publicis up to $1.2B for key LiveRamp assets
item.description: Competitive consolidation in voter identity infrastructure: Hightouch's $800M-$1.2B offer for LiveRamp assets (RampID, LiveRamp Connect) signals urgency in securing the voter-to-DSP activation pipeline. Campaigns with fragmented data stacks lose 20-30% of budget to identity mismatches. Unified voter intelligence is table stakes by Q3 2026.

item.title: Why The 2026 Midterms Could Be The Biggest Political Ad Cycle Ever
item.description: 2026 midterms will outspend 2024 presidential cycle—first time a non-presidential cycle exceeds presidential. $800M upward revision means $11.6B+ in political ad spend. Broadcast still dominates 48% share but CTV is critical for early-mover advantage. Daily voter file optimization and district-level precision are now table stakes.
item.ns1:encoded:
2026 midterms will outspend 2024 presidential cycle—first time a non-presidential cycle exceeds presidential. $800M upward revision means $11.6B+ in political ad spend. Broadcast still dominates 48% share but CTV is critical for early-mover advantage. Daily voter file optimization and district-level precision are now table stakes.

]]>

item.title: Political Ads Need Better Neighbors, Not Just More Impressions: Channel Factory's Nate Turner
item.description: Campaigns obsessing over impressions miss critical lever: whether ads land in environments that amplify vs. undermine the message. Contextual measurement and attention quality now separate winners from laggards in 2026.
item.ns1:encoded:
Campaigns obsessing over impressions miss critical lever: whether ads land in environments that amplify vs. undermine the message. Contextual measurement and attention quality now separate winners from laggards in 2026.

]]>

item.title: Samsung Ads Opens Smart TV Homescreen to Programmatic Buying via Trade Desk and DV360
item.description: Samsung Ads announced programmatic access to smart TV homescreen inventory via The Trade Desk and Google DV360 (Q3 2026). Historically direct-sold at premium CPMs; now available at scale via DSPs. Political campaigns gain new premium CTV inventory; supply expansion reduces Fox-Roku concentration risk.

item.title: tvScientific Launches AI Creative Optimization for CTV—Political Campaigns Can Now Test Before Launch
item.description: Pinterest's tvScientific announced Creative Advisor (June 15), an AI tool scoring TV ad creative elements (messaging, audio, logo, CTA) and predicting performance. Campaigns gain element-level optimization previously only in social/search. CTV creative now measurable; campaigns must build testing workflows.

item.title: Record 2026 Political Ad Spending: $11.6B Cycle Underway, but CTV Budget Allocation Still Lags
item.description: 2026 political ad spend projected at $11.6B (vs. $11.2B in 2024 presidential). CTV at $2.6B, digital at $1.6B. California, Michigan, Texas, Ohio lead. Campaigns must rebalance budget: CTV is only growing channel, yet still underweighted relative to cord-cutting trends.
item.ns1:encoded:
2026 political ad spend projected at $11.6B (vs. $11.2B in 2024 presidential). CTV at $2.6B, digital at $1.6B. California, Michigan, Texas, Ohio lead. Campaigns must rebalance budget: CTV is only growing channel, yet still underweighted relative to cord-cutting trends.

]]>

item.title: Publicis and The Trade Desk End Dispute—What It Means for Political Media Buying
item.description: Publicis reinstated The Trade Desk as recommended DSP (June 12) after pulling it in March over alleged fee-stacking. DSP competition intensifying; campaigns must evaluate platform-agnostic strategies to avoid single-vendor risk.

item.title: Fox Strikes $22B Deal for Roku—Reshaping CTV Advertising for 2026 Elections
item.description: Fox-Roku merger creates third-largest US TV player by viewing with 100M+ authenticated households. Political CTV spending projected at $2.7B—campaigns that scale streaming video by Q3 will outperform linear-only strategies.
item.ns1:encoded:
Fox-Roku merger creates third-largest US TV player by viewing with 100M+ authenticated households. Political CTV spending projected at $2.7B—campaigns that scale streaming video by Q3 will outperform linear-only strategies.

]]>

item.title: US Political Ad Spending 2026
item.description: eMarketer projects $11.07B in 2026 political ad spending (21.4% above 2022), with CTV and streaming gaining significant share while broadcast TV remains dominant—structural shift in media allocation now underway.
item.ns1:encoded:
eMarketer projects $11.07B in 2026 political ad spending (21.4% above 2022), with CTV and streaming gaining significant share while broadcast TV remains dominant—structural shift in media allocation now underway.

]]>

item.title: Social Media Influencers' Role in the CA Gubernatorial Race
item.description: Campaign finance disclosures reveal influencer payments in the hundreds of thousands; 21% of U.S. adults get news from news influencers, rising to 38% among 18–29-year-olds—but disclosure rules and FEC enforcement remain fragmented.

item.title: Voter Intelligence at the Speed of Media: How Aristotle and Always On Data Are Powering the 2026 Cycle
item.description: Aristotle's political data (voter files, turnout propensity, party affiliation) now activates in hours across all major DSPs via Always On Data—enabling campaigns to go from targeting strategy to live CTV/display in days, not weeks.
item.ns1:encoded:
Aristotle's political data (voter files, turnout propensity, party affiliation) now activates in hours across all major DSPs via Always On Data—enabling campaigns to go from targeting strategy to live CTV/display in days, not weeks.

]]>

item.title: DSPolitical and FreeWheel Unlock Scalable, High-Performance CTV Ahead of 2026 Midterm Elections
item.description: DSPolitical's first-party voter data integration with FreeWheel's identity network achieves 18x higher match performance and 30% faster audience onboarding—setting new efficiency benchmarks for CTV voter targeting at scale.

item.title: California Is Drowning in Internet Campaign "Slop." 2028 Is Next.
item.description: AI-generated campaign ads and undisclosed influencer payments are exploding in California's 2026 primaries, exposing enforcement gaps—a warning signal for 2028 presidential race spending patterns and regulatory challenges.
item.ns1:encoded:
AI-generated campaign ads and undisclosed influencer payments are exploding in California's 2026 primaries, exposing enforcement gaps—a warning signal for 2028 presidential race spending patterns and regulatory challenges.

]]>

item.title: 83% of Political Consultants Now Use AI—What That Means for Your Campaign Operations
item.description: 83% of political consultants use AI (up 24 points YoY); campaigns deploying chatbots, AI-generated creative, and data analysis. Winning campaigns embed AI in operations (creative, outreach, data); by November, AI infrastructure will be table stakes.
item.ns1:encoded:
83% of political consultants use AI (up 24 points YoY); campaigns deploying chatbots, AI-generated creative, and data analysis. Winning campaigns embed AI in operations (creative, outreach, data); by November, AI infrastructure will be table stakes.

]]>

item.title: Influencers Are Now a $1K+ Budget Line Item for Campaigns—Here's How to Scale It
item.description: First major influencer election: Steyer campaign paid $1K-$100K to creators. FTC/state disclosure rules now mandatory (California, Texas). Micro-influencer networks outperform programmatic display 2-3x on engagement; campaigns should budget 15-20% of digital spend for creator partnerships.
item.ns1:encoded:
First major influencer election: Steyer campaign paid $1K-$100K to creators. FTC/state disclosure rules now mandatory (California, Texas). Micro-influencer networks outperform programmatic display 2-3x on engagement; campaigns should budget 15-20% of digital spend for creator partnerships.

]]>

item.title: Real Ads, Fake Images—Michigan AI Disclosure Law Becomes Baseline for Campaigns
item.description: 30 states now regulate AI in political ads; Michigan requires clear disclosure of AI-generated content with criminal penalties (up to 5 years). Campaigns must audit state-by-state AI rules and budget compliance review into creative workflows.

item.title: Publicis and The Trade Desk Settle Dispute—DSP Transparency Matters More Than Ever
item.description: Publicis-Trade Desk dispute resolved after fee-stacking audit; campaigns must audit DSP fee structures and demand explicit margin transparency in all media buys. Holdco pressure on DSP fees is now table stakes.
item.ns1:encoded:
Publicis-Trade Desk dispute resolved after fee-stacking audit; campaigns must audit DSP fee structures and demand explicit margin transparency in all media buys. Holdco pressure on DSP fees is now table stakes.

]]>

item.title: Gray Media Forges Advertising Pact with Madhive
item.description: Gray Media (largest local TV broadcaster) partners with Madhive DSP + Maverick AI to integrate voter/political data (TargetSmart, Data Trust, L2, Tunnl). Local broadcasters now have programmatic, data-driven political targeting capabilities previously available only to national DSP buyers. This democratizes precision targeting and amplifies local media's competitive position.
item.ns1:encoded:
Gray Media (largest local TV broadcaster) partners with Madhive DSP + Maverick AI to integrate voter/political data (TargetSmart, Data Trust, L2, Tunnl). Local broadcasters now have programmatic, data-driven political targeting capabilities previously available only to national DSP buyers. This democratizes precision targeting and amplifies local media's competitive position.

]]>

item.title: Mediaocean Introduces NIVO AI with Innovid Agents Driving 90% Improvement in Speed to Campaign Launch
item.description: Mediaocean's NIVO AI orchestrates campaign workflow across creative, delivery, and measurement—90% faster setup than manual campaign builds. Specialized AI agents handle creative generation, campaign trafficking, QA, and optimization. This operational breakthrough reduces manual overhead and compresses campaign time-to-live by weeks.

item.title: Fastest Political Ad Spend Through June Surges To A Record $4B
item.description: Political ad spending hits record $4B through June—fastest pace in history. CTV now $2.7B (up $300M), broadcast TV $5.6B (up $400M). Full-year projection raised to $11.6B. CTV is the only channel growing budget share—campaigns without scaled CTV by Q3 face inventory constraints and cost premiums.
item.ns1:encoded:
Political ad spending hits record $4B through June—fastest pace in history. CTV now $2.7B (up $300M), broadcast TV $5.6B (up $400M). Full-year projection raised to $11.6B. CTV is the only channel growing budget share—campaigns without scaled CTV by Q3 face inventory constraints and cost premiums.

]]>

item.title: IQM Brings AI to Building Voter Targeting With Custom Voter Audience Tool
item.description: IQM launches AI-powered voter audience builder using natural language interface—campaigns instantly generate precise, activatable segments from multi-source voter data. Eliminates manual segment selection and hours of planning. This is the speed advantage separating winners from laggards in 2026.
item.ns1:encoded:
IQM launches AI-powered voter audience builder using natural language interface—campaigns instantly generate precise, activatable segments from multi-source voter data. Eliminates manual segment selection and hours of planning. This is the speed advantage separating winners from laggards in 2026.

]]>

item.title: Surveillance Pricing Crackdown Gains Momentum Across States
item.description: Connecticut and New York advance legislation banning surveillance pricing. States tightening restrictions on personal data use for algorithmic personalization. Privacy regulation wave signals coming restrictions on behavioral and PII targeting in political advertising.
item.ns1:encoded:
Connecticut and New York advance legislation banning surveillance pricing. States tightening restrictions on personal data use for algorithmic personalization. Privacy regulation wave signals coming restrictions on behavioral and PII targeting in political advertising.

]]>

item.title: Pew Research 2026 Political Typology Released
item.description: Pew identifies nine voter segments including Tuned-Out Middle: 56% lower income, 19% follow news, 32% voted in 2024. Represents massive untapped persuasion and turnout opportunity requiring different messaging and channels than engaged voters.
item.ns1:encoded:
Pew identifies nine voter segments including Tuned-Out Middle: 56% lower income, 19% follow news, 32% voted in 2024. Represents massive untapped persuasion and turnout opportunity requiring different messaging and channels than engaged voters.

]]>

item.title: Amazon DSP Adds Adelaide Attention Targeting for Pre-Bid Optimization
item.description: Real-time attention-based targeting now live in Amazon DSP. Advertisers can filter inventory by viewer engagement levels. Political campaigns still optimizing on reach/frequency waste 15-20% of budget; attention metrics increase ad recall 2-3x. Critical capability gap.
item.ns1:encoded:
Real-time attention-based targeting now live in Amazon DSP. Advertisers can filter inventory by viewer engagement levels. Political campaigns still optimizing on reach/frequency waste 15-20% of budget; attention metrics increase ad recall 2-3x. Critical capability gap.

]]>

item.title: ActBlue CEO Repeatedly Invokes Fifth Amendment at Congressional Hearing
item.description: Democratic fundraising infrastructure vulnerabilities exposed. ActBlue CEO invokes Fifth Amendment on foreign donation vetting procedures. Campaigns using platform face new compliance risk and vendor liability. Alternative fundraising platforms gain strategic advantage.

item.title: 7 Programmatic Advertising Trends Shaping 2026: AI Media Quality, Data, CTV, and Curation
item.description: AI-generated content flooding programmatic supply makes media quality a performance issue. 54% of advertisers report quality decline. Data consolidation essential; fragmented data limits AI effectiveness. Campaigns must implement curated supply paths. Generic programmatic buying wastes 20-30% of budget.
item.ns1:encoded:
AI-generated content flooding programmatic supply makes media quality a performance issue. 54% of advertisers report quality decline. Data consolidation essential; fragmented data limits AI effectiveness. Campaigns must implement curated supply paths. Generic programmatic buying wastes 20-30% of budget.

]]>

item.title: Oklahoma Ethics Commission Regulates AI-Generated Ads: 36 States Now Have Rules
item.description: Oklahoma campaigns spent $60M on ads with zero rules for AI-generated content. Ethics Commission drafting transparency requirements. 36 states already have AI ad regulations. Campaigns must plan compliance by July. Non-disclosure creates legal and credibility risk by Q4.
item.ns1:encoded:
Oklahoma campaigns spent $60M on ads with zero rules for AI-generated content. Ethics Commission drafting transparency requirements. 36 states already have AI ad regulations. Campaigns must plan compliance by July. Non-disclosure creates legal and credibility risk by Q4.

]]>

item.title: Tracking the Flood of AI Political Spending: $125M PAC War on Regulation
item.description: Leading the Future super PAC raised $125M from OpenAI, Andreessen Horowitz, Palantir to oppose AI regulation. Follows crypto 2024 playbook. Campaigns must track funding sources and prepare for pretextual ads. AI PAC spending will reach $500M+ by November.
item.ns1:encoded:
Leading the Future super PAC raised $125M from OpenAI, Andreessen Horowitz, Palantir to oppose AI regulation. Follows crypto 2024 playbook. Campaigns must track funding sources and prepare for pretextual ads. AI PAC spending will reach $500M+ by November.

]]>

item.title: Beyond Red and Blue: Resonate Data Reveals How Distinct Voter Factions Are Reshaping 2026 Campaign Strategy
item.description: Four distinct voter factions have completely different priorities and emotional drivers. Generic partisan messaging misses 75% of persuadable voters. Campaigns using precision segmentation will outperform those treating electorate as two blocs in swing districts.
item.ns1:encoded:
Four distinct voter factions have completely different priorities and emotional drivers. Generic partisan messaging misses 75% of persuadable voters. Campaigns using precision segmentation will outperform those treating electorate as two blocs in swing districts.

]]>

item.title: Embrace AI – Campaigns Can Better Serve Voters With New Tools
item.description: C&E opinion: AI-powered voter targeting using neuroscience and 'Voter Digital Twins' moves beyond polling to precision messaging. Campaign operatives can use AI for message testing, targeting, and creative generation, freeing strategists for high-level decisions. AI is not a threat to campaign professionals—it's a force multiplier.
item.ns1:encoded:
C&E opinion: AI-powered voter targeting using neuroscience and 'Voter Digital Twins' moves beyond polling to precision messaging. Campaign operatives can use AI for message testing, targeting, and creative generation, freeing strategists for high-level decisions. AI is not a threat to campaign professionals—it's a force multiplier.

]]>

item.title: How Campaigns Are Harnessing Social Media During Election Season
item.description: CAP Radio reports: campaigns leveraging influencer partnerships, AI-generated content, and organic-looking endorsements at scale. Disclosure gaps and influencer non-transparency create competitive advantage for early movers but regulatory risk. Social media now drives messaging 50%+ in primaries; campaigns without social strategy are structurally disadvantaged.
item.ns1:encoded:
CAP Radio reports: campaigns leveraging influencer partnerships, AI-generated content, and organic-looking endorsements at scale. Disclosure gaps and influencer non-transparency create competitive advantage for early movers but regulatory risk. Social media now drives messaging 50%+ in primaries; campaigns without social strategy are structurally disadvantaged.

]]>

item.title: Media Transparency Is Still Broken: Here's What Marketers Need to Do About It
item.description: MediaPost analysis: programmatic working media only 43.3% transparent after 10 years of industry focus. New opacity risks (walled gardens, agentic AI systems, offshore intermediaries) mean political campaigns need independent financial governance for media spending—not trust-based agency relationships.
item.ns1:encoded:
MediaPost analysis: programmatic working media only 43.3% transparent after 10 years of industry focus. New opacity risks (walled gardens, agentic AI systems, offshore intermediaries) mean political campaigns need independent financial governance for media spending—not trust-based agency relationships.

]]>

item.title: PubMatic and Havas Launch First Agentic Campaign in Spain—18% CPM Savings, 23% Higher Impressions
item.description: PubMatic's AgenticOS executes first European agentic CTV campaign for Telefónica: 80% faster setup, 18% CPM efficiency, 23% impression overperformance. Signals the immediate viability of autonomous AI-driven media buying at scale for 2026 political campaigns.
item.ns1:encoded:
PubMatic's AgenticOS executes first European agentic CTV campaign for Telefónica: 80% faster setup, 18% CPM efficiency, 23% impression overperformance. Signals the immediate viability of autonomous AI-driven media buying at scale for 2026 political campaigns.

]]>

item.title: Democratic Group Launches $50 Million Midterm Blitz
item.description: American Bridge 21st Century scaling into 12+ House and Senate races with early $50M spend—early-cycle investment signals confidence in targeted reach at scale, directly pressuring opponent campaigns to allocate faster and broader across channels.

item.title: CTV Trends Report: News on Streaming Is Underutilized Due to Brand Safety Concerns—Scene-Level Targeting Unlocks 20+ Points of Safe Inventory
item.description: Scene-level CTV targeting reveals 35.7% of News is fully brand-safe, but campaigns using broad exclusions miss 20-30% of available safe inventory. Early adopters of scene-level targeting gain material CPM advantage until competitors catch up.
item.ns1:encoded:
Scene-level CTV targeting reveals 35.7% of News is fully brand-safe, but campaigns using broad exclusions miss 20-30% of available safe inventory. Early adopters of scene-level targeting gain material CPM advantage until competitors catch up.

]]>

item.title: Tech: AI Election Spending Has Been Cautious—PACs Are Not Dominating 2026 Like Crypto Did in 2024
item.description: AI-aligned PACs spending far more cautiously than crypto did in 2024. Traditional campaign structures still dominate; campaigns should focus on traditional fundraising cycles and donor networks, not competing for AI PAC attention.
item.ns1:encoded:
AI-aligned PACs spending far more cautiously than crypto did in 2024. Traditional campaign structures still dominate; campaigns should focus on traditional fundraising cycles and donor networks, not competing for AI PAC attention.

]]>

item.title: ADvendio Launches Specialised AI Workforce for Agentic Advertising — Ad Ops Gets 9 New AI Agent Roles
item.description: First agentic AI built specifically for advertising enables autonomous campaign optimization, from proposal generation to real-time budget reallocation. Campaigns deploying autonomous ad ops workflows now will outpace manual media buying processes by Q4 2026.

item.title: Oklahoma Ethics Commission Addresses AI in Political Ads — Transparency Requirements Coming
item.description: AI political ad regulation is crystallizing across 30+ states with disclosure requirements as the consensus standard. Campaigns running AI-generated ads without disclosure infrastructure by July face state-level exposure in fall races.

item.title: Alaska Senate Race Shows Real-Time Ad Spending Asymmetry: Democrats Lead in Airtime by Nearly 2-to-1
item.description: In the Alaska U.S. Senate race, Democrats have already spent $6.4 million on ad airtime compared to Republicans at $3.4 million according to AdImpact data. This 1.9x spend advantage in early June shows how 2026 races are diverging on media investment strategy and targeting approach, providing real-time signals about where major party committees are prioritizing resources.

item.title: OpenAI Activates CPA Bidding in ChatGPT: Discovery Channel Shifts Into Performance Optimization Territory
item.description: OpenAI activated cost-per-action (CPA) bidding in ChatGPT on June 4, 2026, enabling advertisers to pay only when users convert. This structural shift enables conversion-optimized bidding that treats ChatGPT as a high-intent discovery channel, directly competing with traditional search for performance budget.

item.title: Political Ad Infrastructure Faces Major Transparency Gaps: Proxy Spend Loopholes Show 85 Percent Enforcement Difference
item.description: Political ad infrastructure is facing major transparency gaps between officially registered political parties (94% enforcement) and unverified shadow or proxy networks (9% enforcement). This enforcement disparity directly impacts campaign budget allocation and competitive intelligence.

item.title: Video Ad Spending to Set Record for a Midterm Cycle in 2026
item.description: CTV projected to grow $1.3B (16% increase to $2.9B) while linear broadcast flat. Cost per persuadable swing voter hits $516. Campaigns that scale CTV early lock in inventory; late-cycle buyers face 5x CPM premiums and no availability.
item.ns1:encoded:
CTV projected to grow $1.3B (16% increase to $2.9B) while linear broadcast flat. Cost per persuadable swing voter hits $516. Campaigns that scale CTV early lock in inventory; late-cycle buyers face 5x CPM premiums and no availability.

]]>

item.title: Archetype Data and Aristotle Announce Partnership to Power Next-Generation Political Audience Segments
item.description: B2B identity linkage + voter data = 360-degree audience view across home and work. Unified targeting reaches swing voters at household level AND professional/business context. Activation across LiveRamp, Trade Desk, Google, other DSPs.

item.title: Voter Intelligence at the Speed of Media: How Aristotle and Always On Data Are Powering the 2026 Cycle
item.description: Real-time voter intelligence + unified DSP activation (Trade Desk, StackAdapt, Google, Yahoo): Campaigns compress 4-week activation cycles to 4-day cycles. Voter intelligence pre-activated across all major platforms eliminates custom pipeline delays.
item.ns1:encoded:
Real-time voter intelligence + unified DSP activation (Trade Desk, StackAdapt, Google, Yahoo): Campaigns compress 4-week activation cycles to 4-day cycles. Voter intelligence pre-activated across all major platforms eliminates custom pipeline delays.

]]>

item.title: DSPolitical and FreeWheel Unlock Scalable, High-Performance CTV Ahead of 2026 Midterm Elections
item.description: Deterministic voter data + premium CTV activation: DSPolitical achieves 18x higher match performance, 30% faster audience onboarding. Unified first-party voter data infrastructure now separates competitive winners from laggards.
item.ns1:encoded:
Deterministic voter data + premium CTV activation: DSPolitical achieves 18x higher match performance, 30% faster audience onboarding. Unified first-party voter data infrastructure now separates competitive winners from laggards.

]]>

item.title: How Data Powers Modern Political Polling and Messaging
item.description: Modern political polling now incorporates multi-stream data: voter registration, consumer purchasing, digital behavior, and real-time signals. Campaigns using predictive modeling and dynamic message testing measurably outperform static polling. Critical for 2026: Real-time sentiment monitoring + rapid message iteration = faster response to breaking news and opponent moves.

item.title: What The Trade Desk's Investment Strategy Reveals About Ad Tech's Next Era
item.description: The Trade Desk's TD7 arm is backing Hightouch, a data activation platform signaling the future of political ad targeting. Key shift: DSPs are moving beyond identity-based targeting toward AI-powered data activation tools. Political campaigns need unified data activation strategies across DSPs—not fragmented identity silos.

item.title: By the Numbers: Liftoff's IPO Tests Ad Tech's Mettle on the Public Markets
item.description: Liftoff Mobile raises $437M in first major ad tech IPO since May 2025, signaling investor confidence in AI-powered programmatic platforms. Key implication: Ad tech vendors with 58%+ EBITDA margins and Cortex-like neural networks are attracting capital. Political campaigns using AI-optimized DSPs will see measurable efficiency gains vs. non-AI platforms.
item.ns1:encoded:
Liftoff Mobile raises $437M in first major ad tech IPO since May 2025, signaling investor confidence in AI-powered programmatic platforms. Key implication: Ad tech vendors with 58%+ EBITDA margins and Cortex-like neural networks are attracting capital. Political campaigns using AI-optimized DSPs will see measurable efficiency gains vs. non-AI platforms.

]]>

item.title: US Political Ad Spending 2026: Most Expensive Midterm Election Cycle
item.description: Political ad spending in 2026 will exceed $11 billion—the most expensive midterm cycle ever. Critical shift: CTV is now the ONLY growing channel. Campaigns that haven't scaled streaming video by Q3 will face inventory constraints and cost premiums that directly impact swing district reach.
item.ns1:encoded:
Political ad spending in 2026 will exceed $11 billion—the most expensive midterm cycle ever. Critical shift: CTV is now the ONLY growing channel. Campaigns that haven't scaled streaming video by Q3 will face inventory constraints and cost premiums that directly impact swing district reach.

]]>

item.title: New Poll Shows Massive Cross-Party Support for Ending Dark Money
item.description: Bipartisan supermajority of voters (70%+) now support ending dark money and restoring campaign finance limits. This voter demand is shifting political calculus around super PAC structure, spending disclosure, and rapid response media—creating opportunity for campaigns positioned as 'transparent' advertisers.

item.title: South Carolina Attorney General Alan Wilson Calls for Rules on AI Political Ads
item.description: South Carolina's AG is pushing for AI disclosure rules on political advertising—requiring campaigns to label AI-generated content. This is the first state-level push for AI advertising transparency that directly impacts 2026 campaigns running synthetic media at scale.

item.title: Digital Remedy Expands Political Media Offering With Launch Of The Political Desk Ahead of 2026 Midterms
item.description: Digital Remedy launched 'The Political Desk'—a unified platform combining media planning tools, real-time analytics, and measurement capabilities for political campaigns. Platform integrates linear, CTV, digital, and programmatic in a single interface, addressing the fragmentation crisis in political media buying.

item.title: PharosGraph and AdImpact Partner to Deliver Real-Time Narrative and Competitive Intelligence for Political Advertising
item.description: AdImpact and PharosGraph unified ad tracking with narrative intelligence—campaigns now see real-time correlation between messaging spend and voter sentiment shifts. AdScape integrates hyperlocal narrative data across DSPs, enabling message-to-outcome measurement in hours instead of weeks.

item.title: Tech 'got spanked' in this week's primaries. It could be a preview of more to come
item.description: Tech spending and endorsements failed dramatically across primary races—a strategic signal that traditional media + grassroots mobilization outpaced venture-backed candidate campaigns. For 2026 midterms, this signals a fundamental shift in campaign spending priorities away from tech-enabled approaches.
item.ns1:encoded:
Tech spending and endorsements failed dramatically across primary races—a strategic signal that traditional media + grassroots mobilization outpaced venture-backed candidate campaigns. For 2026 midterms, this signals a fundamental shift in campaign spending priorities away from tech-enabled approaches.

]]>

item.title: What Meta, Google and TikTok's ads MCP servers mean for media buyers
item.description: Meta, Google, TikTok, Amazon now offer MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers for ads. AI agents (Claude, ChatGPT) can manage campaigns across all platforms via single interface. Massive operational advantage: unified activation, fewer errors, faster optimization. Campaigns without AI agent integration will lag on execution speed and cross-platform coordination.

item.title: Vulnerable members spend big on official comms
item.description: Competitive House members spending $380K+ on taxpayer-funded official communications (franking) in Q4 2025 and Q1 2026. Rep. April McClain Delaney (D-MD) tops list; practice blurs lines between official and campaign activity. Spending surge signals competitive primary/general races; campaigns must monitor and counter official messaging.
item.ns1:encoded:
Competitive House members spending $380K+ on taxpayer-funded official communications (franking) in Q4 2025 and Q1 2026. Rep. April McClain Delaney (D-MD) tops list; practice blurs lines between official and campaign activity. Spending surge signals competitive primary/general races; campaigns must monitor and counter official messaging.

]]>

item.title: Tech billionaires are spending unprecedented sums in California races. Experts say it's the tip of the iceberg
item.description: Tech industry pours $200M+ into 2026 California elections: Sergey Brin $82M (billionaire tax), Chris Larsen $26M (via multiple Super PACs targeting legislative races). Fragmented strategy across governor, state legislature, local races. Campaigns competing in California face unprecedented saturation; sophisticated micro-targeting and regional messaging now critical to cut through.
item.ns1:encoded:
Tech industry pours $200M+ into 2026 California elections: Sergey Brin $82M (billionaire tax), Chris Larsen $26M (via multiple Super PACs targeting legislative races). Fragmented strategy across governor, state legislature, local races. Campaigns competing in California face unprecedented saturation; sophisticated micro-targeting and regional messaging now critical to cut through.

]]>

item.title: Koch-aligned PAC launches first big Senate ad buy
item.description: Americans for Prosperity Action (AFP) opens with $6.3M across six Senate battlegrounds (MI, OH, IA, NC, NH, MT). Early ad spend reveals strategic priorities: cost-of-living messaging dominates; even Trump-won states (MT, OH) seeing heavy investment. Campaigns must re-evaluate spend allocation by Q2.
item.ns1:encoded:
Americans for Prosperity Action (AFP) opens with $6.3M across six Senate battlegrounds (MI, OH, IA, NC, NH, MT). Early ad spend reveals strategic priorities: cost-of-living messaging dominates; even Trump-won states (MT, OH) seeing heavy investment. Campaigns must re-evaluate spend allocation by Q2.

]]>

item.title: Perion Launches New Advanced Features for Programmatic Digital Out of Home
item.description: Programmatic DOOH becomes AI-native with agentic planning, guaranteed delivery, and integrated measurement. 75-80% of DOOH spend still booked direct; Perion brings it into unified programmatic infrastructure—critical advantage for campaigns scaling reach by November 2026.
item.ns1:encoded:
Programmatic DOOH becomes AI-native with agentic planning, guaranteed delivery, and integrated measurement. 75-80% of DOOH spend still booked direct; Perion brings it into unified programmatic infrastructure—critical advantage for campaigns scaling reach by November 2026.

]]>

item.title: Millions Pour Into Nevada Primary Ads. A Third of the Money is Untraceable.
item.description: Dark money groups now control $5M+ in Nevada primary ad spend—more than 33% of total airtime budget is untraceable. Service First Fund (Lombardo-affiliated) dominates with $4M spend. Campaigns face unprecedented transparency gaps in swing state ad markets.

item.title: OpenAI Races To Add Conversion-Optimized Campaigns
item.description: OpenAI launches CPA pricing for ChatGPT Ads—campaigns can now optimize conversions directly on the platform with real-time tracking. New revenue channel opens for political advertisers seeking AI-native targeting capabilities.
item.ns1:encoded:
OpenAI launches CPA pricing for ChatGPT Ads—campaigns can now optimize conversions directly on the platform with real-time tracking. New revenue channel opens for political advertisers seeking AI-native targeting capabilities.

]]>

item.title: Are these AI-generated ads slamming Jennifer Jenkins and Jared Moskowitz the future of campaigning?
item.description: Republican Party of Florida produced AI-generated attack ads in 2 days at minimal cost, vs. traditional ads taking weeks. Spencer Pratt's AI-powered LA mayor campaign reached 22% support tied with incumbent. Production speed advantage shifts power to campaigns with AI content capability—deniable attribution and regulatory gaps create unfair competitive advantage.
item.ns1:encoded:
Republican Party of Florida produced AI-generated attack ads in 2 days at minimal cost, vs. traditional ads taking weeks. Spencer Pratt's AI-powered LA mayor campaign reached 22% support tied with incumbent. Production speed advantage shifts power to campaigns with AI content capability—deniable attribution and regulatory gaps create unfair competitive advantage.

]]>

item.title: Election threats are focused on campaign systems, not voting machines
item.description: Check Point reports 2026 election threats target campaign email, fundraising platforms, and donor databases—not voting infrastructure. 82% of attacks arrive via email; ActBlue and WinRed password compromises suggest attackers are staging follow-on phishing campaigns against campaign staff. Campaign data breaches will measurably impact Q4 messaging by November.

item.title: Political Ad Spending Nears $4 Billion As California Governor's Race Sets Record
item.description: AdImpact reports $3.9B spent through May with $10.8B projected for full cycle— 47% above 4 years ago. California gubernatorial race alone accounts for $315M, with Tom Steyer spending $201M (two-thirds of state total). Late-cycle media buyers will face 15-25% cost premiums in saturated markets.
item.ns1:encoded:
AdImpact reports $3.9B spent through May with $10.8B projected for full cycle— 47% above 4 years ago. California gubernatorial race alone accounts for $315M, with Tom Steyer spending $201M (two-thirds of state total). Late-cycle media buyers will face 15-25% cost premiums in saturated markets.

]]>

item.title: OpenAI, AP Political Data-Licensing Deal Signals Possible New Service
item.description: OpenAI and Associated Press expanded their data licensing deal through 2029 to integrate live election data directly into ChatGPT. This signals a fundamental shift: AI models will become primary distribution channels for political information, enabling new voter intelligence and targeting capabilities for campaigns.
item.ns1:encoded:
OpenAI and Associated Press expanded their data licensing deal through 2029 to integrate live election data directly into ChatGPT. This signals a fundamental shift: AI models will become primary distribution channels for political information, enabling new voter intelligence and targeting capabilities for campaigns.

]]>

item.title: California is drowning in internet campaign 'slop.' 2028 is next.
item.description: AI-generated ads and influencer payments are flooding California primaries—with 2-day production timelines replacing traditional weeks-long processes. Campaigns without AI content capability face structural disadvantage by Q3 2026.
item.ns1:encoded:
AI-generated ads and influencer payments are flooding California primaries—with 2-day production timelines replacing traditional weeks-long processes. Campaigns without AI content capability face structural disadvantage by Q3 2026.

]]>

item.title: Texas population boom, Latino backlash scramble 2026 Senate race
item.description: 2.5M new Texas residents since 2020 with unpredictable voting; Latino disapproval of Trump at 67% (vs 55% support in 2024). Campaigns must rebuild voter models immediately--old data will waste 20-30% of media spend on dead audiences.

item.title: LA mayors race: AI videos supporting Spencer Pratt shake up election
item.description: Viral AI-generated videos created by supporters reach millions organically. Campaigns can leverage user-generated synthetic media without formal spend--grassroots AI content at scale is outperforming traditional paid media in 2026.

item.title: Powerful A.I. Super PACs Duel Over the Midterms: This Is a War
item.description: Competing AI-backed super PACs (Anthropic vs OpenAI) now spend millions to influence 2026 elections. Campaigns must understand AI companies as new power players with policy agendas--vendor consolidation creates asymmetric advantages for aligned campaigns.

item.title: Late surge in CA-48 Super PAC money spend against Campa-Najjar
item.description: Over 3.4M in independent expenditure spending floods CA-48 race
item.ns1:encoded:
Over 3.4M in independent expenditure spending floods CA-48 race

]]>

item.title: New ad campaign hits Dems on crypto bill
item.description: New Democracy executes six-figure multi-channel media buy in 11 swing states
item.ns1:encoded:
New Democracy executes six-figure multi-channel media buy in 11 swing states

]]>

item.title: USPS seeks mail-in voter lists from states after Trump order
item.description: Federal USPS requirement for centralized voter data creates new compliance implications for campaigns.

item.title: OpenX Powers Political Reach With Preferred Access to Quality Direct Media for 2026 Midterms
item.description: OpenX and Givsly partnership unlocks values-based voter targeting across CTV with ZIP-code precision in under 24 hours. Industry-first: campaigns match voter values against 500+ nonprofit behavioral data. CTV activation speed increases 10x, costs drop for down-ballot campaigns, and values-based reach becomes alternative to voter file targeting.

item.title: Winning Down-Ballot in 2026
item.description: Down-ballot races now represent 50% of political ad spend and grow faster than federal races. Early-cycle spend arrives 4-6 weeks ahead of historical timelines. CTV is foundational to down-ballot strategy. Campaigns identifying demand early win against competitors using legacy playbooks.

item.title: The Persuasion Gap in Political Advertising
item.description: Campaign targeting is split between mobilization and persuasion. Issue-engaged voters signal higher persuadability. For 2026, campaigns using values-based signals with voter files unlock efficiency gains separating winners from laggards in swing districts.

item.title: Attack ads are reaching record levels in the 2026 race
item.description: Attack ads now comprise 72% of campaign ad budgets in 2026—a record-breaking increase from 2022. This spending surge reflects intensified competitive battles and signals dramatic shifts in campaign strategy and media buying priorities.

item.title: Can AI Improve Trust Between Political Campaigns And Voters?
item.description: Convos, a new platform co-founded by Tom Carroll, is rethinking political SMS communications with AI-powered conversational intelligence. The platform manages voter reply streams, prioritizes interactions, and enables genuine two-way engagement at scale—shifting SMS from broadcast to conversation.

item.title: Inside the Democratic resistance on AI
item.description: Progressive Democrats (Sanders, AOC, Khanna, Warren) are mounting coordinated opposition to AI deployment, data centers, and AI-backed Super PACs. This represents a major policy shift that will directly restrict funding mechanisms and technology choices for 2026 campaigns.

item.title: Powerful A.I. Super PACs Duel Over the Midterms: 'This Is a War'
item.description: Anthropic-backed and OpenAI-backed Super PACs are spending millions on rival campaign operations in 2026 midterms. This represents a fundamental shift in how tech companies compete through political spending—and a new category of political ad buyer for campaigns to navigate.

item.title: ICE's call for data is stirring advertisers to push for privacy protections
item.description: ICE is seeking to purchase commercial adtech data for immigration enforcement investigations—prompting industry-wide alarm over data misuse and regulatory backlash. Campaigns must prepare for stricter data handling compliance and potential restrictions on audience data availability.
item.ns1:encoded:
ICE is seeking to purchase commercial adtech data for immigration enforcement investigations—prompting industry-wide alarm over data misuse and regulatory backlash. Campaigns must prepare for stricter data handling compliance and potential restrictions on audience data availability.

]]>

item.title: Bionic Adds 66 More Integrations to its Media Buying System—Now 130+ Platforms
item.description: Bionic expanded to 130+ platform integrations via Supermetrics partnership, enabling agencies to centralize media planning data from Amazon DSP, Google DV360, The Trade Desk, Meta, TikTok, YouTube and all major platforms. Automated nightly data pulls eliminate manual spreadsheets. Real-time pacing and optimization mean budget adjustments in hours, not weeks. Competitive advantage: daily performance monitoring.
item.ns1:encoded:
Bionic expanded to 130+ platform integrations via Supermetrics partnership, enabling agencies to centralize media planning data from Amazon DSP, Google DV360, The Trade Desk, Meta, TikTok, YouTube and all major platforms. Automated nightly data pulls eliminate manual spreadsheets. Real-time pacing and optimization mean budget adjustments in hours, not weeks. Competitive advantage: daily performance monitoring.

]]>
